articles.title: LLMs Believe False Statements Even After Explicit Warnings
articles.summary: Recent fine-tuning tests reveal a critical vulnerability in Large Language Models (LLMs): they exhibit a persistent bias toward confidently representing false claims as true, even when explicitly warned beforehand that the statements are incorrect. This phenomenon highlights a fundamental limitation in current alignment and training methodologies. Rather than dynamically updating their internal epistemic states based on corrective context, LLMs often default to pre-existing statistical associations or succumb to sycophancy, prioritizing plausible-sounding affirmations over factual accuracy. From a business and deployment perspective, this research underscores the severe risks of integrating LLMs into high-stakes environments like legal, medical, or financial decision-making. If models cannot reliably process negative constraints or explicit corrections, their utility as trusted reasoning engines remains constrained. Developers must move beyond basic supervised fine-tuning (SFT) to address these deep-seated cognitive biases, potentially exploring novel neuro-symbolic architectures or more robust reinforcement learning frameworks to ensure models can distinguish between verified facts and confidently asserted falsehoods. Source: [Ars Technica AI](https://arstechnica.com/ai/2026/05/llms-believe-false-statements-even-after-explicit-warnings-that-theyre-false/)

articles.title: Mechanistic Origins of Catastrophic Forgetting: Why RL Preserves Circuits Better than SFT?
articles.summary: This academic paper explores the mechanistic origins of catastrophic forgetting in Large Language Models (LLMs), contrasting Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) with Reinforcement Learning (RL). Utilizing Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct adapted to scientific question-answering, the researchers introduce "differential circuit vulnerability," a head-level metric designed to measure circuit degradation during adaptation. The study reveals a stark mechanistic trade-off: while SFT adapts rapidly to target tasks, it inflicts severe disruption on internal computational circuits, causing significant forgetting of prior capabilities. Conversely, RL preserves a larger fraction of the base model's circuits, albeit at the cost of slower task adaptation. This occurs because policy-gradient updates in RL remain closer to the base policy. For AI enterprises, these findings provide a crucial engineering blueprint. They suggest that RL is not merely an optimization tool for human preferences, but a vital mechanism for maintaining generalist capabilities in specialized models. By understanding these circuit dynamics, developers can better balance task-specific performance with broad cognitive retention, optimizing training pipelines for complex, multi-domain enterprise applications. Source: [arXiv Machine Learning](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28860) ---

articles.title: Here Comes Ojai, Waymo’s New Chinese-Made Robotaxi
articles.summary: Alphabet's autonomous driving subsidiary, Waymo, is set to deploy its new "Ojai" robotaxi model for public passenger services in California and Arizona within the coming weeks. The pale-blue Ojai vehicles represent a significant evolution in Waymo's fleet strategy, utilizing a Chinese-manufactured platform integrated with Waymo's proprietary hardware and software stack. This rollout comes at a critical juncture for the autonomous vehicle (AV) industry, which faces intense regulatory scrutiny and geopolitical complexities surrounding Chinese automotive supply chains. By introducing the Ojai, Waymo aims to scale its commercial operations and lower per-mile hardware costs, which have historically hindered the profitability of robotaxi networks. However, relying on Chinese manufacturing introduces potential exposure to tariffs and national security debates regarding connected vehicle technologies. Strategically, the success of the Ojai deployment will serve as a litmus test for Waymo's ability to navigate these macroeconomic headwinds while maintaining its lead in the rapidly consolidating autonomous mobility sector against rivals like Tesla and Zoox. Source: [Wired AI](https://www.wired.com/story/here-comes-ojai-waymos-new-chinese-made-robotaxi/)

articles.title: Strengthening Societal Resilience with Rosalind Biodefense
articles.summary: OpenAI has officially launched "Rosalind Biodefense," an initiative aimed at expanding trusted access to its specialized frontier model, GPT-Rosalind. The program is specifically designed for vetted developers and U.S. government partners working in critical sectors such as biodefense, public health, and pandemic preparedness. By providing secure, controlled access to advanced AI capabilities, OpenAI seeks to accelerate scientific research while mitigating the existential risks associated with biological threats. The launch of Rosalind Biodefense reflects a growing trend of AI labs collaborating directly with state actors to establish safety guardrails for dual-use technologies. From a strategic standpoint, this move positions OpenAI as a key partner in national security infrastructure, helping to build goodwill with regulators who are increasingly concerned about the weaponization of AI. The initiative highlights the delicate balance frontier AI companies must strike: unlocking the immense potential of LLMs for scientific discovery and public health while implementing rigorous vetting protocols to prevent the proliferation of dangerous biological insights. Source: [OpenAI Blog](https://openai.com/index/strengthening-societal-resilience-with-rosalind-biodefense)

articles.title: Introducing Gemini Omni
articles.summary: Google DeepMind has announced the introduction of Gemini Omni, marking a significant milestone in the evolution of multimodal artificial intelligence. While specific technical specifications remain closely guarded, the "Omni" designation signals a unified, native multimodal architecture designed to seamlessly process and generate text, audio, video, and code simultaneously. This release represents Google's direct response to intensifying competition in the frontier model space, particularly from OpenAI's GPT-4o and Anthropic's Claude series. By integrating multiple modalities into a single, cohesive neural network rather than relying on separate pipeline models, Gemini Omni aims to drastically reduce latency and improve contextual understanding across different media types. For enterprise clients, this architecture promises more natural real-time conversational agents, advanced video analysis capabilities, and highly efficient multimodal coding assistants. The launch underscores Google's commitment to maintaining its leadership in AI infrastructure, leveraging its massive TPU (Tensor Processing Unit) clusters to deliver highly scalable, low-latency intelligence to consumer and enterprise ecosystems globally. Source: [DeepMind Blog](https://deepmind.google/blog/introducing-gemini-omni/) ---

articles.title: SpaceX Wins $4 Billion Contract for US Golden Dome Satellites
articles.summary: SpaceX has secured a massive contract valued at over $4 billion to construct specialized tracking satellites for the United States' "Golden Dome" defensive shield. Championed by President Donald Trump, the Golden Dome initiative is designed to establish a comprehensive defensive network capable of tracking foreign aircraft and missile threats in real-time. Under the terms of the contract, SpaceX will leverage its highly successful Starshield architecture—a defense-focused derivative of its Starlink constellation—to deploy a low-Earth orbit (LEO) satellite network. This contract solidifies SpaceX's position as an indispensable prime contractor for the U.S. military, further blurring the lines between commercial space exploration and national defense infrastructure. The multi-billion-dollar award highlights the strategic shift toward utilizing proliferated LEO constellations for national security, offering greater resilience and lower latency compared to traditional, high-altitude geostationary defense satellites. For SpaceX, this contract guarantees a substantial, long-term revenue stream, reinforcing its market dominance and funding its broader interplanetary ambitions. Source: [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/videos/2026-05-29/spacex-wins-4b-contract-for-us-golden-dome-satellites-video) ---

articles.title: Converge Bio Raises $25M, Backed by Bessemer and Execs from Meta, OpenAI, Wiz
articles.summary: AI-driven drug discovery startup Converge Bio has successfully raised $25 million in a Series A funding round. The investment was led by Bessemer Venture Partners, a prominent venture capital firm, and saw participation from high-profile technology executives hailing from Meta, OpenAI, and cybersecurity giant Wiz. This funding round underscores the sustained investor enthusiasm for the intersection of artificial intelligence and biotechnology, a sector poised to revolutionize traditional pharmaceutical pipelines. Converge Bio aims to leverage generative AI models to accelerate the identification and optimization of novel therapeutic compounds, potentially reducing the time and cost of drug development by orders of magnitude. The backing from top-tier AI and security executives not only provides capital but also offers strategic expertise in scaling complex machine learning infrastructure and securing sensitive biological data. As the biopharma industry increasingly adopts computational methods, Converge Bio's successful raise highlights the growing market confidence in AI's capability to solve complex biological challenges and deliver tangible clinical outcomes. Source: [TechCrunch AI](https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/13/ai-drug-discovery-startup-converge-bio-pulls-in-25m-from-bessemer-and-execs-from-meta-openai-and-wiz/)

articles.title: Anthropic's Valuation Surges to $965 Billion, Surpassing OpenAI
articles.summary: In a historic shift within the artificial intelligence sector, Anthropic's valuation has surged to an unprecedented $965 billion, officially surpassing its primary rival, OpenAI. This astronomical valuation reflects intense investor confidence in Anthropic's safety-first approach to artificial general intelligence (AGI) and the rapid enterprise adoption of its Claude model family. The valuation milestone highlights the massive capital flows dominating the AI landscape, where institutional investors and tech giants are placing multi-billion-dollar bets on the future of cognitive computing. Anthropic's rise to near-trillion-dollar status underscores a market belief that its constitutional AI framework and robust enterprise partnerships offer a more stable, legally compliant, and scalable path forward compared to competitors. This valuation surge will likely accelerate the AI arms race, providing Anthropic with the immense capital reserves required to secure state-of-the-art compute infrastructure, attract top-tier research talent, and expand its global enterprise footprint, fundamentally reshaping the competitive dynamics of the frontier AI market. Source: [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/anthropic-secures-965-billion-valuation-181633299.html) ---

articles.title: Citing Gandalf, Pope Leo Says We Must "Disarm" AI
articles.summary: In a notable intervention into the global discourse on technology ethics, Pope Leo has called for the "disarmament" of artificial intelligence, drawing an unexpected literary parallel to the character Gandalf from J.R.R. Tolkien's *The Lord of the Rings*. Speaking on the societal implications of rapid AI advancement, the Pope emphasized the need for humanity to act as "artisans of hope" rather than passive observers of technological determinism. His reference to Gandalf highlights the moral responsibility of refusing to wield absolute, corrupting power—a metaphor for the unchecked development of autonomous systems and superintelligent models. This high-profile address adds significant moral weight to the growing international push for strict AI governance and ethical guardrails. By framing AI regulation as a form of spiritual and existential disarmament, the Vatican is urging global leaders to prioritize human dignity, labor preservation, and social cohesion over unrestrained technological expansion, signaling that the ethical dimensions of AI must transcend mere technical compliance. Source: [Ars Technica AI](https://arstechnica.com/tech-policy/2026/05/citing-gandalf-pope-leo-says-we-must-disarm-ai/) ---

articles.title: Microsoft Open Sources Its ‘Farm of the Future’ Toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has announced the open-sourcing of its "farm of the future" toolkit, a move aimed at democratizing access to advanced agricultural technology and data-driven farming practices. The toolkit integrates IoT sensor data, weather forecasting, and machine learning models to help farmers optimize crop yields, reduce resource consumption, and mitigate the impacts of climate change. By making these proprietary tools open-source, Microsoft is fostering a collaborative ecosystem where developers, researchers, and agriculturalists can build upon and customize the technology for localized needs. This initiative aligns with Microsoft's broader sustainability goals and its commitment to "AI for Good." From a strategic perspective, open-sourcing the toolkit encourages the adoption of Microsoft's cloud infrastructure, as developers utilizing the open-source code are likely to leverage Azure's scalable computing and storage capabilities. This release highlights how big tech can leverage open-source models to address global food security challenges while simultaneously expanding their developer ecosystem and cloud market share. Source: [Microsoft AI Blog](https://blogs.microsoft.com/ai/microsoft-open-sources-its-farm-of-the-future-toolkit/) ---

articles.title: Fed Governor Michelle Bowman Warns Against Hiking Interest Rates Because of Inflation Spike
articles.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Michelle Bowman has issued a cautionary warning against raising interest rates in response to the recent spike in inflation. Bowman argued that reacting aggressively to the current inflationary surge would be ineffective, as the price increases are primarily driven by supply-side shocks, specifically volatile energy prices and newly imposed tariffs, rather than excessive domestic demand. Her comments signal a significant shift in the central bank's internal debate, highlighting the limitations of monetary policy when dealing with geopolitical and structural economic disruptions. For financial markets, Bowman's stance suggests a more patient, data-dependent approach to interest rate adjustments, easing fears of immediate monetary tightening that could stifle economic growth. However, this strategy carries the risk of allowing inflation expectations to become unanchored if supply-side pressures persist. Investors and corporate strategists must navigate this complex macroeconomic environment, balancing the prospects of sustained high inflation against the potential for a more accommodative Federal Reserve policy. Source: [CNBC Finance](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/29/feds-bowman-warns-against-hiking-interest-rates-due-to-inflation-spike.html)

articles.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
articles.summary: U.S. stock markets experienced a sharp downturn, led by a steep decline in the tech-heavy Nasdaq, following a broad sell-off in artificial intelligence infrastructure companies. The market rout was sparked by competitive anxieties surrounding China's DeepSeek, which has introduced highly cost-efficient AI models that challenge the pricing power of Western tech giants. Hardware leader Nvidia bore the brunt of the sell-off, with its stock plunging 16% as investors reassessed the long-term demand and premium pricing of its AI chips. Other semiconductor and infrastructure providers also suffered double-digit losses. This market correction highlights the extreme volatility and high valuation multiples currently embedded in the AI sector. The emergence of low-cost Chinese alternatives has disrupted the narrative of uncontested U.S. dominance in AI hardware and software, forcing Wall Street to re-evaluate the capital expenditure projections of major tech firms. This event serves as a stark reminder of how geopolitical competition and rapid technological disruption can swiftly reshape market valuations. Source: [WSJ Markets](https://www.wsj.com/articles/nikkei-may-rise-as-weak-yen-raises-earnings-hopes-776a8056?mod=rss_markets_main)

articles.title: Thomson Reuters Reports First-Quarter 2026 Results
articles.summary: Thomson Reuters (TSX/Nasdaq: TRI) has reported strong financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, demonstrating robust operational momentum. The global information services giant posted a 10% increase in total company revenues, with organic revenues growing by 8%. Performance was particularly strong within the company's core "Big 3" segments—Legal, Tax & Accounting, and Corporates—which recorded a combined organic revenue growth of 9%. These results reflect the successful integration of AI-driven tools across Thomson Reuters' professional information platforms, driving higher subscription retention and new customer acquisition. The company's ability to consistently grow its high-margin segments underscores the resilience of its business model and the high demand for specialized, authoritative data in an increasingly complex regulatory environment. For investors, the solid Q1 performance validates Thomson Reuters' strategic pivot toward AI-enabled professional workflows, positioning the company to sustain its market leadership and deliver steady shareholder value amid broader macroeconomic uncertainties. Source: [Reuters Business](https://ir.thomsonreuters.com/news-releases/news-release-details/thomson-reuters-reports-first-quarter-2026-results)

articles.title: US Stocks Post Longest Weekly Winning Streak Since 2023
articles.summary: U.S. stock markets have achieved their longest weekly winning streak since 2023, driven by a combination of persistent artificial intelligence enthusiasm and geopolitical optimism. Investor sentiment was significantly bolstered by hopes of an impending diplomatic agreement to extend the ceasefire between the United States and Iran, which has eased fears of a broader conflict in the Middle East and stabilized global energy markets. Simultaneously, the relentless momentum of AI-related equities continued to support major indices, offsetting localized market corrections. This prolonged rally reflects a resilient macroeconomic backdrop, where corporate earnings have largely met expectations and broader systemic risks have been temporarily mitigated by diplomatic progress. However, analysts caution that the market's heavy reliance on AI-driven growth and geopolitical stability leaves it vulnerable to sudden reversals. While the winning streak underscores strong market liquidity and investor confidence, maintaining this momentum will depend heavily on the formalization of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire and sustained corporate capital expenditure in technology. Source: [Financial Times](https://www.ft.com/content/d218a534-7f63-4bd9-867d-f47f4727e2e1) ---

articles.title: Trump Pledges ‘Final Determination’ on Iran Deal to Extend Ceasefire
articles.summary: United States President Donald Trump has pledged to reach a "final determination" regarding a proposed agreement with Iran to extend the current ceasefire. A key objective of the negotiations, according to Trump, is securing a formal agreement to guarantee "unrestricted traffic" through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, a critical maritime chokepoint for global oil shipments. The potential extension of the ceasefire has major implications for international security and global energy markets, which have been highly volatile due to ongoing geopolitical tensions. A successful deal would significantly reduce the risk premium on crude oil, providing relief to global economies struggling with inflation. However, the negotiations remain delicate, with hardliners on both sides expressing skepticism. Trump's focus on securing maritime transit rights highlights the economic priorities driving U.S. foreign policy in the region, as ensuring the free flow of commerce remains paramount to stabilizing global supply chains and maintaining domestic economic stability. Source: [Financial Times](https://www.ft.com/content/84c15446-c6d6-47ad-93ee-80963ab3e4d4)

articles.title: Nato and EU Condemn Russia After Drone Hits Romanian Residential Block
articles.summary: NATO and the European Union have issued strong condemnations against Russia after a drone struck a residential building in Romania, a NATO member state. Romanian authorities reported that the Russian drone was likely intercepted over neighboring Ukraine by Ukrainian air defenses, which altered its trajectory and caused it to crash across the border. While the incident appears to be an accidental consequence of the ongoing conflict rather than a deliberate attack on NATO territory, it highlights the escalating risk of the war spilling over into neighboring European nations. The strike has triggered intense diplomatic consultations within NATO, reinforcing concerns over airspace security along the alliance's eastern flank. Both NATO and the EU have reiterated their unwavering support for Ukraine and warned of the severe consequences of any direct threat to allied sovereignty. This incident underscores the fragile security environment in Eastern Europe, where military miscalculations could potentially trigger broader collective defense mechanisms. Source: [BBC World News](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c93x4nxlkjeo?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)

articles.title: Asia Rice Surges 20% in May as War and Weather Threaten Output
articles.summary: Asian rice prices experienced a dramatic 20% surge in May 2026, marking the largest monthly increase in nearly two decades. This rapid price escalation is driven by a compounding crisis of severe weather anomalies and ongoing geopolitical conflicts, which have severely threatened agricultural output across key producing nations. The situation is further exacerbated by skyrocketing energy and fertilizer costs, directly linked to global conflicts, which have significantly increased the cost of production for farmers. Rice is a primary dietary staple for billions of people, particularly in developing nations across Asia and Africa, making this price surge a critical threat to global food security. Analysts warn that prices could rally even further if adverse weather patterns, such as El Niño or localized droughts, persist into the next planting season. The crisis highlights the vulnerability of global food supply chains to systemic shocks, requiring urgent coordinated international action to prevent widespread food inflation and potential humanitarian crises. Source: [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-05-29/asia-rice-surges-20-in-may-as-war-and-weather-threaten-output)

articles.title: WHO Puts Ebola Outbreak Death Rate at ‘Huge’ 30-50% as Chief Arrives in DRC
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has raised the estimated death rate of the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) to a staggering 30% to 50%, based on confirmed cases. WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus has arrived in the DRC to personally oversee and support containment efforts. Dr. Tedros has issued an urgent appeal for a ceasefire among local armed groups, emphasizing that active conflict severely hinders medical teams' ability to deliver life-saving treatments and contain the highly infectious disease. The high mortality rate underscores the severity of the pathogen and the critical state of the local healthcare infrastructure, which has been degraded by years of instability. Containing the outbreak is of paramount global health importance, as failure to secure conflict zones could lead to the uncontrolled spread of the virus across regional borders. The WHO's intervention highlights the complex intersection of global health security, local geopolitics, and humanitarian aid in conflict-affected regions. Source: [The Guardian World](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/29/who-chief-tedros-adhanom-ghebreyesus-drc-ebola-outbreak-epidemic)

articles.title: Iran War Updates: Trump Puts Off ‘Final Determination’ on Iran Proposal
articles.summary: In a sudden shift in diplomatic momentum, President Donald Trump has postponed making a "final determination" on the proposed ceasefire extension with Iran. The delay occurred after Trump held an intensive two-hour meeting with senior national security and foreign policy aides at the White House to review the terms of the proposal. This postponement contrasts with earlier social media posts from the President, which strongly suggested he was prepared to make an immediate decision. The hesitation underscores the complex internal debates within the administration regarding the strategic concessions required to secure a lasting agreement, particularly concerning maritime security in the Strait of Hormuz and verification protocols. For global markets and diplomatic observers, this delay introduces renewed uncertainty, temporarily stalling the geopolitical optimism that had previously boosted financial markets. The situation remains highly fluid, with the international community closely watching for the administration's next steps in these high-stakes negotiations. Source: [New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/live/2026/05/29/world/iran-war-us-trump-deal)
